pulls.title: Add draft-mcguinness-oauth-token-exchange-cnf-00
pulls.author: mcguinness
pulls.body:
## Summary

Initial `-00` of **Confirmation Response Parameter for OAuth 2.0 Token Exchange**, written in kramdown-rfc (matching the repo's build tooling). Replaces the `i-d-template` placeholder.

Defines a `cnf` response parameter for OAuth 2.0 Token Exchange ([RFC 8693]) so a client can verify that sender-constraint binding (DPoP, mTLS) was applied **without inspecting the issued token** — covering opaque tokens, encrypted tokens, and the `token_type=N_A` case where the existing binding signal is unavailable.

## Contents

- Confirmation Response Parameter + comparison rules vs. the in-token `cnf` claim
- Authorization Server and Client behavior (fail-closed on absent `cnf`)
- `token_exchange_cnf_response_supported` AS metadata
- Examples (DPoP / mTLS / no-constraint), Security Considerations, IANA registrations

## Build

Builds clean through `kramdown-rfc` → `xml2rfc --text` with zero warnings; all cross-references and the 8 normative/informative references resolve.

[RFC 8693]: https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc8693
